name: Kids' Play Recipes
recipeIngredient: 2 to 3 tablespoons washable tempera paint (powdered or liquid tempera work equally well)
recipeInstructions:
Mega-Bubble Mix
1. Mix all ingredients (stir, don't shake) in a large bucket.
2. Collect an assortment of bubble wands including colanders, spatulas, even hangers - anything with holes that can withstand being dunked in water.
3. Head outdoors and bombard the neighborhood with bubbles!
Sidewalk Chalk
1. Prepare your chalk molds by covering one end of each tissue tube with duct tape or masking tape.
2. Pour warm water into a mixing container, then sprinkle the plaster powder into the water until it no longer dissolves (about 1 1/2 cups). Stir thoroughly - until the mixture is as thick as pudding.
3. Add tempera paint to the plaster mixture and stir until blended.
4. Pour the colored plaster into the tissue tubes. Gently tap each tube to get rid of air bubbles.
5. Let dry for one to two days, peel off the mold, and chalk up a masterpiece!
Fingerpaint
1. In a small bowl or measuring cup, mix the gelatin with 1/4 cup cold water. Set aside to soak.
2. Put cornstarch and 3/4 cup cold water in a saucepan and stir until dissolved. Add hot water and cook over medium heat (with parental help) - stirring non-stop - until the mixture begins to boil and looks clear. Remove from heat.
3. Blend in the softened gelatin. Then add soap flakes and stir until the paint is free of lumps.
4. Set aside to cool. Divide into five jars or cups. Add food coloring drop by drop until you get a color you like. Wear an old shirt or smock to protect your clothes.
Gloop
1. Pour glue into a large bowl. Add water and stir until the glue and water blend. Add paint, and stir more until mixed well.
2. In a small bowl or measuring cup, mix warm water and borax powder until the powder dissolves. Slowly pour this mixture into the glue mixture, stirring constantly for two minutes.
3. Knead the sticky gloop with your hands until it's smooth and stretchy. Store in an airtight container.
Face paint
1. Mix shortening, cornstarch, and flour until it forms a paste.
2. Add glycerin and stir again until the mixture becomes smooth and spreadable.
3. Add food coloring a drop at a time until you get the desired color.
4. Smooth a dab of cold cream on the child's face before painting. Use cotton swabs to create designs such as a sun, heart, football, daisy, etc. Homemade face paint washes off with soap and water.
All projects are non-edible. Supervise children so they don't taste their concoctions.
